steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The passage lists three ordered steps, marking the first with "First" and the third with "Third." The blank introduces the middle item, so it needs a transition that signals the second item in a numbered sequence.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'For example,': signals that what follows is a specific instance of a general claim. Forming a hypothesis is not an example of researching a question — wrong logical relationship. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Second,': signals the next item in an ordered list, completing the First $\Rightarrow$ Second $\Rightarrow$ Third structure the passage establishes. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Therefore,': signals a logical consequence. Forming a hypothesis is presented as a distinct step, not a result that follows from the previous step — wrong logical relationship. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Instead,': signals contrast or replacement, implying the second step contradicts or replaces the first. That reverses the passage's intended sequential meaning. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
